turns.content: Had a contractor come look at it. I've been wanting to finish it out. Rec room. Whole thing was planned out. He gets down there, starts poking around, and finds foundation cracks. Not small ones either.
Foundation cracks will stop a project cold. How bad are we talking — cosmetic settling cracks or structural?
Structural. He brought in a foundation guy who confirmed it. Horizontal cracking along the east wall. Water intrusion too. Said it needs to be addressed before anybody does anything else down there.
Horizontal cracking with water intrusion is serious — that's lateral pressure from the soil, not just normal settling. Did they give you a number for the repair?

40 grand. That's just the foundation work. That's before I spend a dime on finishing the space. So you take the 40 and add what I was going to spend on the buildout and it's not even close to worth it.
Yeah, $40K just to get back to baseline before the actual project starts — that math doesn't work for a rec room. That's a tough pill. How long had you been planning this?
Months. Had the whole layout figured out. Pool table, TV setup, bar area. Marie gave me the green light and everything. That never happens. And now it's dead.

Getting the green light from your wife and then having the house itself veto the plan — that's a special kind of frustrating. It's not like you changed your mind or couldn't afford it. The foundation literally said no.

That's exactly what it is. The house said no. Listen I've been in buildings my whole career. I know what cracked foundations turn into if you ignore them. So I'm not going to be stupid about it. But I'm not spending 40 thousand dollars to fix something I didn't even know was broken just so I can then spend more money on top of it.

That's the right call even though it's a lousy one. You're not going to throw good money after bad, and you know better than most what structural issues lead to if they're left alone. Does the foundation work need to happen regardless at some point, or can it wait as long as you're not finishing the space?

The guy said it's not urgent. Could go years without getting worse if we manage the water. But it's not getting better either. I'll deal with it eventually. Just not now. And not so I can put a pool table down there.

At least it's not an emergency — that's the one piece of good news in this. Monitor the water, keep an eye on the cracks, and deal with it on your timeline, not under pressure. The rec room idea was a good one. It's just not this house's time for it.
That project's dead. I'm not revisiting it. I told Marie and she took it better than I did honestly. She said at least now I can stop talking about it.
Ha. Marie sounds like she knows how to read the situation. At least you found out now instead of halfway through demo when you're already committed. Saves you a much bigger headache.
Yeah. Could have been worse. Alright. Just needed to vent. That's done.
Heard. Sometimes you just need to say it out loud and close the book. Sorry it didn't work out.
